recommendations: add and run a focused MCP smoke test suite (import each exposed endpoint and call with minimal valid args) in CI
deduplicate endpoint names in the plugin manifest (multiple `parse_arguments/main` collisions) by namespacing with module prefixes
expose higher-level task-oriented MCP tools (e.g.
`run_bamCoverage`
`run_computeMatrix`) instead of raw internal helpers to reduce misuse risk
add strict input/output schemas with pydantic models per tool (file existence
extension
numeric bounds
enum validation)
improve error mapping in `adapter.py` to return actionable user-safe messages (dependency missing
bad BAM index
invalid chromosome names)
pin and verify runtime dependencies in `mcp_output/requirements.txt` against `pyproject.toml` (numpy/scipy/matplotlib/pysam/pyBigWig compatibility matrix)
add a reproducible test-data mini bundle for MCP tests (tiny BAM/BW + expected outputs) and run on Linux/macOS
introduce contract tests comparing MCP-executed outputs vs native CLI outputs for key commands (bamCoverage/computeMatrix/plotHeatmap)
add timeout and resource guards for heavy commands (max threads
temp dir quotas
graceful cancellation)
implement structured logging and per-request tracing IDs in `mcp_service.py` for debuggability
cache argparse/parser construction for repeated calls to reduce overhead
document endpoint stability levels and deprecation policy in `README_MCP.md`
add security hardening for file path inputs (normalize/resolve
block path traversal
optional allowlisted workspace root)
include a preflight dependency check endpoint (`health/deps`) to surface missing binary/library issues early
add golden-path examples in README for common workflows (BAM→bigWig
matrix→heatmap)
expand CI matrix to multiple Python versions supported by deepTools and include optional dependency-on/off modes
create fallback execution mode for commands that are safer via subprocess CLI when import-side effects are detected
add version handshake endpoint reporting deepTools + plugin versions for compatibility checks
and add regression tests for known edge cases (empty chromosomes
zero-coverage bins
malformed BED/GTF)

success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Low intrusiveness risk and high import feasibility (0.88) made import-mode adapter generation viable.
Repository has clear command-module structure (multiple CLI-oriented modules with parse/main patterns), which is suitable for MCP wrapping.
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio, indicating baseline operational viability.
failure_reasons: Quality gap: original project tests did not pass in this run context (passed=false), reducing confidence in behavioral parity.
DeepWiki analysis failed, so enrichment/context from that subsystem was unavailable.
Endpoint design issues (name collisions such as repeated parse_arguments/main) create functional ambiguity and downstream integration risk.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; repository imported via zip fallback (81 files detected). Timing per node not separately reported.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully with structural, dependency, complexity, and risk outputs. DeepWiki sub-analysis failed but did not block.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; 7 MCP output files generated, import-mode adapter selected.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health check passed; original project test stage did not pass in this execution context. Total workflow duration ~349.48s.
